title: Types should avoid having members with visibility set higher than the type's visibility
description: Types should not have members with visibility set higher than the type's visibilityAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearmaintainabilityCode SmellconfusingWhy is this an issue?There’s no point in having apublicmember in a non-publictype because objects that can’t access the type will never have the chance to access the member.This rule raises an issue when a type has methods, fields, or inner types with higher visibility than the type itself has.Noncompliant code exampleinternal class MyClass { public static decimal PI = 3.14m; // Noncompliant public int GetOne() // Noncompliant { return 1; } protected record NestedType // Noncompliant: outer class is internal { public bool FlipCoin() // Noncompliant: outer class is internal { return false; } // ... } }Compliant solutionpublic class MyClass
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Check if a type has members with visibility higher than the type itself (e.g., public members in internal class)

conditions: Type has restricted visibility (internal, private, etc.)
Member has public or protected visibility
Member visibility is broader than containing type visibility

never_trigger_on: Public types (can have any member visibility)
logic.reasoning: There's no point in having a public member in a non-public type because objects that can't access the type will never be able to access the member. This confuses API consumers and indicates a design issue.

should_trigger: internal class Service { public void Method() { } }
internal class Service { public string Name { get; set; } }
private class Helper { public int Value; }
should_not_trigger: public class Service { public void Method() { } }
internal class Service { internal void Method() { } }
